source_jobs.title: Financial Controller (Part-time)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ALGO1

We’re a VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for the retail industry. Our platform is designed from the ground up - no legacy, no patchwork systems - just a clean slate and a clear vision. Our mission is to bring the intelligence of modern machine learning directly to the in-store shopping experience.

We are taking an almighty swing of the bat; it is difficult, full of risk, and exactly why we are here.

 


THE ROLE

We're looking for a Financial Controller to work closely with our leadership team to build and run the finance function that will scale with algo1. We're a VC-backed startup redefining in-store shopping through personalisation and retail media, and as we grow, we need someone who can bring order, control, and rigour to an increasingly international group.

You'll own core financial operations end to end while building the processes, controls, and systems that let a fast-moving company scale without breaking.

 


WHAT YOU WILL OWN

 - The month-end and year-end close process across the including management accounts, consolidated reporting, and board/investor packs

 - Cash flow forecasting, burn and runway modelling, treasury, and intercompany transactions across the group

 - Payroll across jurisdictions (UK PAYE, and international staff via EOR), alongside accounts payable, expenses, and the spend approval process

 - Statutory compliance across the group — Companies House filings, annual accounts, VAT, corporation tax, R&D tax credit claims, and US filings in conjunction with external advisors

 - Relationships with auditors, external accountants, tax advisors, and legal counsel

 - The design and continuous improvement of financial controls, policies, and processes as we scale

 


ESSENTIAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - Fully qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent)

 - Proven experience as a Financial Controller or finance professional

 - Solid experience with UK statutory accounts, compliance, and governance, including hands-on Companies House filings

 - Experience preparing management accounts and reporting for boards

 - Comfortable working autonomously across a broad remit and building processes from scratch

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Exposure to multi-entity or international group structures (particularly UK/US)

 - Experience with payroll across multiple jurisdictions

 - Experience with R&D tax credit claims

 - Experience with modern accounting and HR/payroll software (e.g. Xero, QuickBooks, Rippling)

 - Familiarity with share option schemes (particularly EMI) and cap table platforms (e.g. Carta)

 - Experience supporting fundraising processes, due diligence, or data room preparation

 

If you’re excited by the idea of shaping the future of retail and eager to make a real impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Fullstack Engineer (Frontend)
source_jobs.description:
About algo1

We’re a VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for the retail industry. Our platform is designed from the ground up — no legacy, no patchwork systems — just a clean slate and a clear vision. Our mission is to bring the intelligence of modern machine learning directly to the in-store shopping experience.



The Role

We’re looking for a Full Stack Engineer with Frontend specialism to work closely with product, design, mobile, and machine learning team members to design & build experiences that re-define retail shopping. You'll help help build engaging & intuitive web experiences using highly responsive UI components and real-time data flows.



We are looking for people of varying experience levels who are willing to take the lead on delivering features end-to-end. You will have already embraced agentic coding and how it can be used to enhance individual and team productivity.



What You Will Own

 - Design and implement reactive, real-time web interfaces that help manage & redefine what is possible with in-store content personalisation

 - Understand the different users of the platform and advocate for their needs

 - Translate user and business needs into a functional web application using RBAC and ReBAC to cater to the different users

 - Contribute to API and broader system design & documentation as part of end-to-end delivery of features



Essential Qualifications

 - Strong understanding of UX principles and web design fundamentals

 - Expert in Typescript and React

 - Experience with real-time and bi-directional communication protocols and patterns (eg. WebSockets, SSE)

 - Built and deployed services on any major cloud providers' services (Azure, GCP, AWS)

 - AI-Native development: Fast iteration & prototyping using AI tools such as Copilot, Claude or Cursor



Nice to Have

 - Experience in backend development and event-driven architecture
   
   - Our stack includes python, FastAPI, postgres, Kafka and MQTT

 - Worked with IoT or Edge-oriented architectures

 - Experience or knowledge in retail POS, inventory, or loyalty systems



If you’re excited by the idea of shaping the future of retail and eager to make a real impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About algo1

We’re a VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for the retail industry. Our platform is designed from the ground up - no legacy, no patchwork systems - just a clean slate and a clear vision. Our mission is to bring the intelligence of modern machine learning directly to the in-store shopping experience.

We are taking an almighty swing of the bat; it is difficult, full of risk, and exactly why we are here.



The Role

We’re looking for a Full Stack Engineer to work closely with product, design, mobile, and machine learning team members to design & build experiences that re-define in-store shopping. We believe that agentic, LLM-driven workflows are here to stay and are looking for engineers who have embraced this and will being a different perspective to our product development lifecycle.

You'll help build engaging & intuitive web experiences and the event-driven & real-time backend services that power them. You’ll work across the stack, implementing highly responsive UI components and real-time data flows and APIs on a backend designed for scale & flexibility.



What You Will Own

 - Design and implement reactive, real-time web interfaces that help manage & redefine what is possible with in-store content personalisation

 - Contribute to distributed, event-driven backend services connecting our web experience with information from remote devices and external data sources.

 - Model and manage structured and semi-structured data across storage layers

 - Integrate AI-driven personalisation and real-time insights into user flows

 - Contribute to overall system design: service boundaries, data ownership, scaling, and observability



Essential Qualifications

 - Built modern full-stack applications (Python/Java/Go + React/ts)

 - Worked with a variety of storage technologies (eg. Postgres, Mongo, Redis, Object)

 - Experience with async and bi-directional communication protocols and patterns (eg. WebSockets, SSE)

 - Designed and implemented event-driven distributed systems (eg. Kafka/Pulsar, RabbitMQ, MQTT, or similar)

 - Actively utilising and experimenting with AI development tools (eg. Claude, Copilot, Cursor) to speed up iteration, prototyping and overall development



Nice to Have

 - Built and deployed services on any major cloud providers' services (Azure, GCP, AWS)

 - Experience with container orchestration and service containerisation (Kubernetes, Docker)

 - Worked with real-time data collection and/or processing

 - Worked in an IoT or Edge-oriented architectures

 - Experience or knowledge in retail POS, inventory, or loyalty systems



If you’re excited by the idea of shaping the future of retail and eager to make a real impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Staff AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About algo1

We’re a VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for the retail industry. Our platform is designed from the ground up — no legacy, no patchwork systems — just a clean slate and a clear vision. Our mission is to bring the intelligence of modern machine learning directly to the in-store shopping experience.



The Role

You will be an expert in agentic development familiar with both the latest advancements in models as well as the agentic toolchain and best practices. You will work closely with product, design, mobile, and machine learning team members to design & build agents that drive everything from user experience to platform automation and efficiency. Alongside product development, you will bring your expertise to enable the rest of the company to deliver faster and more effectively, whilst maintaining quality, using the latest agentic approaches and tools.



What You Will Own

 - Defining and implementing complex, high-impact agentic AI platform solutions.

 - Driving best practices and enablement for agentic development across the engineering organization.

 - Establishing and maintaining robust evaluation (Evals) strategies for LLM agents, focusing on performance, latency, quality, and explainability.

 - Keeping up with the latest developments in models (Frontier Labs and Open Source) as well as agent development toolchains and best practices.

 - Deployment, scaling, monitoring, and cost-efficiency of agentic applications.



Essential Qualifications

 - Deep understanding and hands-on experience with modern agentic development frameworks (e.g. Pydantic AI).

 - Familiarity with designing and implementing complex, multi-step agentic workflows and planning/reasoning capabilities.

 - Proven ability to design and implement robust evaluation (Evals) strategies for large language model (LLM) agents, including metrics for performance, latency, and quality.

 - Expertise in advanced context management techniques, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), memory structures, and context window optimization

 - Experience with deploying and monitoring agentic applications in production environments including scaling, monitoring (OTEL), cost, and token usage.



Nice to Have

 - Experience in backend development and event-driven architecture

 - Our stack includes Python, FastAPI, postgres, Kafka and MQTT

 - Worked with IoT or Edge-oriented architectures

 - Experience or knowledge in retail POS, inventory, or loyalty systems



If you’re excited by the idea of shaping the future of retail and eager to make a real impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Why We Exist

Grocery retail is among the industries that are best positioned to benefit from AI. Retailers create huge volumes of data across a plethora of domains (Customer, Supplier, Store, Staff, Energy, Transport, Supply Chain etc.), but today most grocers come nowhere close to using this data to its full potential. Retailers today are united in their belief that AI will define the next era of competitiveness yet most have only scratched the surface of its potential.



algo1 was born to correct this.



Mission matters to us. There are plenty of great startups that can give you autonomy, pace, and the feeling of being in it together. What makes us different is how we obsess over grocery and the shopper. If you want to change an experience that touches everyone, this is the right place.



About the Role

This is a role that blends curiosity and execution: you imagine how in-store retail could feel, code it with AI, and get it in front of shoppers. You rapidly iterate, observing shoppers as you test prototyped features, looking at how they respond, then deciding if the feature makes the cut. Successful features are handed to engineering to review, refine and commit to the live product, all at pace.

How you build is key to success too. We're agentic-first, we've built our own coding harness, and everyone works on max plans, so you'll never be rate-limited. The only limit will be your ability to imagine what shoppers truly value.



What You'll Do

 - Own an idea end to end, from a concept to a proven feature.

 - Research market trends, shopper behaviour, and competitor offerings to formulate initial concepts.

 - Code a feature and iterate rapidly, observing shoppers as you test prototyped features.

 - Evaluate shopper feedback and sentiment to determine if a feature makes the cut.

 - Provide Engineering a validated feature so they can build the durable version.
   
   

About You

The person who thrives in this role will blend an instinct for what shoppers desire with the agentic coding skill to build their ideas fast. We are a startup and that inevitably means a high-intensity environment. We're looking for capability, drive and a desire to make a meaningful difference. The right person will always have been a bit obsessive about the things they care about.

This role sits under our Head of B2C Product. It's a great fit for someone who wants to bridge coding and product: a product role at heart, but built for an exceptional coder who wants to be close to customers and help solve real problems.



Office Is Our Default

We get our energy from being together, and this is primarily an office based role. It will suit someone who loves the quick conversations and the connection of building something together in a fast paced environment. We're on what we think is the best garden square in London, a couple of minutes from Tottenham Court Road, so it's an easy place to get to and a good place to be.



What We Offer

 - Backed by leading VCs, we pay competitive salaries for top talent and give meaningful.

 - 25 days' annual leave plus bank holidays. equity.

source_jobs.title: Staff Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
STAFF FULL STACK ENGINEER

We’re a VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for the retail industry. Our platform is designed from the ground up, no legacy, no patchwork systems, just a clean slate and a clear vision. Our mission is to bring the intelligence of modern machine learning directly to the in-store shopping experience.

 

We are taking an almighty swing of the bat; it is difficult, full of risk, and exactly why we are here.

 


THE ROLE

As a Staff Full Stack Engineer, you’ll work at the intersection of Product, ML, and Engineering to design and build the retail stack of the future; imagine AI driven content delivery on autonomous edge devices on an IoT network, combined with the backend infrastructure of a cutting edge adTech platform. This isn't a staff role where you "work across the stack"— you'll be an integral part of defining the stack.

 


WHAT YOU WILL OWN

 - System Architecture: Design and lead the implementation of distributed, event-driven services that bridge the gap between cloud intelligence and edge devices.

 - Technical Strategy: Set the standards for service boundaries, data ownership, and system observability to ensure we scale seamlessly from 1 to 1,000+ locations.

 - AI Integration: Architect the orchestration layer for LLM-driven agentic workflows, moving beyond simple API calls to deeply integrated, autonomous system behaviors.

 - Mentorship & Culture: Continuously raise the bar for engineering excellence. Conduct deep design reviews, mentor mid-level engineers, and champion a culture of "building with AI" and the use of autonomous development agents to accelerate our velocity.

 


ESSENTIAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - Track Record: 8+ years of experience, with recent years spent in a Staff, Lead, or Principal capacity at a high-growth startup or a sophisticated scale-up.

 - Full-Stack Mastery: Expert-level proficiency in modern backends (Python, Go, or Java) and high-performance frontends (React/TypeScript).

 - Distributed Systems: Experience designing and operating event-driven architectures at scale (e.g., Kafka, Pulsar, or MQTT).

 - Real-time Protocols: Mastery of async real-time, bi-directional communication protocols and patterns whether that's (WebSockets, gRPC, SSE, MQ) for low-latency data flows.

 - The "AI-First" Mindset: You don't just use AI tools; you have optimised your entire workflow around and can teach others to do the same.

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Edge & IoT: Experience with "Cloud-to-Edge" orchestration and managing state across geographically distributed systems.

 - Containerised Workloads: High comfort level with Kubernetes and container orchestration in a CI/CD-heavy environment.

 - Domain Expertise: Prior exposure to the complexities of large scale retailTech, adTech, or marTech platforms.



If you’re excited by the idea of shaping the future of retail and eager to make a real impact from day one, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Head of Hardware
source_jobs.description:
About algo1

algo1 is an AI-native platform reinventing the in-store shopping experience. Founded by Simon Franks (LoveFilm, Redbus) and backed by leading venture investors, we're ~40 people building from our London office.

Our devices live in retail stores and get used, abused, and relied on every day by real shoppers. They have to be tough enough to survive that and beautiful enough that people want to touch them.



The Role

We're hiring a Head of Hardware to own the physical product end-to-end, from today's early units through retail trials to mass deployment. You will define that strategy from scratch rather than inherit one.

You'll work directly with the founders and our two product leaders (B2C and B2B), backed by a strong engineering support in Pakistan and external design partners you'll select and direct.

The core challenge: consumer-grade quality and a premium finish at low cost, at scale. If you default to commoditised components over custom engineering and can prove it saved cost or time, we'll get along.



What You Will Own

 - The hardware product end-to-end: From today's early units through retail trials and into large-scale deployment.

 - Sourcing and supplier strategy: manufacturer selection, sourcing and management.

 - Supply chain scaling: including certification, production ramp-up, logistics, and successful deployment into retail environments.

 - External design partners: You will brief, select, and direct industrial design and engineering contractors.

 - The supply chain ramp: certification, production scale-up, logistics and getting devices into stores ready to work.
   
   

You're Right For This Role If You've

 - Taken a physical product from prototype through EVT/DVT/PVT into scaled production.

 - Shipped by coordinating design, engineering, and manufacturing through outsourced relationships, ideally with direct design-to-manufacturing experience in China.

 - Owned ODM and contract manufacturing relationships and survived real supply chain disruption.

 - Built consumer-grade hardware for demanding settings (retail, payments, logistics, mobility) where drop testing, IP ratings, and cleaning regimes are second nature.
   
   

The Details

 - London-based, in person; we build together.

 - Competitive pay plus meaningful equity.

 - Full autonomy over hardware strategy, working directly with the founders.



We would love it hear from you if you've shipped products people rely on every day and they just keep working,

source_jobs.title: Graduate Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHY WE EXIST

Grocery retail is one of the industries best positioned to benefit from AI. Retailers generate enormous volumes of data across every part of their business, from customers and suppliers to stores, staff, energy, transport, and the supply chain. Yet, most grocers come nowhere close to using this data to its full potential. Retailers are united in their belief that AI will define the next era of competitiveness, yet most have only scratched the surface.

algo1 was born to correct this.

Mission matters to us. There are plenty of great startups that offer autonomy, a fast pace, and the feeling of building something together. What makes us different is our obsession with grocery and the shopper. If you're excited by the idea of changing an experience that touches almost everyone, this is the right place.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a graduate role reporting to our Head of B2C Product, working closely with our engineering team.

This role blends curiosity with execution. You'll imagine how the in-store grocery experience could be made better, use AI to prototype those ideas, and get them in front of shoppers quickly. You'll iterate rapidly, watching how shoppers respond with features you prototype and deciding which ideas are worth pursuing. Features that prove themselves are handed over to engineering to review, refine, and commit to the live product, all at pace.

How you build matters as much as what you build. We're agentic-first, we've built our own coding harness, and everyone works on max plans, so you'll never be rate-limited. The only limit will be your ability to imagine what shoppers truly value.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own an idea end to end, from initial concept to a proven feature.

 - Research market trends, shopper behaviour, and competitor products to inform new concepts.

 - Build features using AI and iterate rapidly, testing prototypes with real shoppers.




ABOUT YOU

You have been building things for as long as you can remember, and most of them nobody asked you to build. There's a trail behind you: side projects, code you wrote at midnight to solve a problem only you had, or something you shipped that real people ended up using. At some point, you went far deeper than what the assignment required simply because you couldn't leave it alone. That instinct is what we're hiring for.

You can use agentic coding tools to turn ideas into working features quickly, and you have strong instincts for what shoppers actually want

We're a startup, so this is a high-intensity environment. The people who thrive here throw themselves into the things they care about.




OFFICE IS OUR DEFAULT

We get our energy from being together, so this is primarily an office-based role. It will suit someone who loves the quick conversations and building something together at pace. We're on what we think is the best garden square in London and a couple of minutes from Tottenham Court Road. It's easy to get to and a good place to be.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Backed by leading VCs, we pay competitive salaries for top talent and give meaningful equity.

 - 25 days' annual leave, plus public holidays.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
WHY WE EXIST

At algo1, we're solving some of grocery's hardest problems, the kind that have eluded even the world's most sophisticated retailers.

We begin with the in-store grocery trip, an experience that touches billions of lives every week. By combining frontier AI, exceptional product design, and a deep understanding of shopper behaviour, we're creating an experience that feels effortless, personal, trusted and quietly magical.

We ask a great deal of ourselves because our mission matters. We know that plenty of startups offer autonomy, pace and exceptional colleagues. What makes us different is our obsession with the grocery shopper. Every decision begins with them.

If you want to design an experience that millions of shoppers could use every week, solve genuinely difficult product and interaction challenges, and help create something that becomes part of everyday life, you'll feel at home here.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role is for someone with exceptional taste and a deep appreciation for craft.

Working alongside our Head of Design, you'll help shape the experiences shoppers interact with every day. Every screen has to earn attention. Grocery is a fast-moving environment where people make decisions in seconds and rarely give an interface their full focus.

You'll help evolve the design system at the heart of our product while designing and shipping real customer experiences. You'll shape the foundations that everyone else builds upon and help influence the direction of both the product and the brand.

We work agentic-first. The time between an idea, a prototype and real shopper feedback is measured in days, not months. You'll test your work with real shoppers in real stores, learning from behaviour rather than opinion.

Designers here have a remarkable amount of influence. We don't hand over finished requirements. We discover the product together with engineers, product leaders and shoppers. The best ideas win, regardless of where they come from.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design shopper-facing experiences from initial idea through to shipped product.

 - Use motion intentionally to guide attention where it matters and stay quiet where it doesn't.

 - Prototype rapidly and test with real shoppers in real stores.

 - Work closely with engineering so what ships matches what was designed, down to the pixel.

 - Help evolve the design system as the product grows.

 - Use AI as part of your everyday design process to explore ideas, prototype faster and improve your craft.




ABOUT YOU

We're a startup, and that inevitably means intensity. We're looking for capability, curiosity, drive, and a genuine desire to make a meaningful difference.

The right person is naturally obsessive about the details most people overlook. You don't need grocery experience but you do need to be excited by standing in a store and watching how people actually shop.

This is a hands-on craft role reporting to our Head of Design. You'll spend your time designing rather than managing, with the opportunity to learn from someone who has set an exceptionally high bar.




WHAT GREAT LOOKS LIKE


ESSENTIAL

 - Around 3-5 years' experience designing digital products.

 - Experience working within and evolving a design system, including components, tokens, patterns and documentation.

 - Strong UX skills, with the ability to simplify complex problems into intuitive experiences.

 - Exceptional visual craft across layout, typography, colour, spacing and visual hierarchy.

 - Confident prototyping to communicate and validate ideas.

 - AI is already part of how you design.

 - Comfortable working in ambiguity and moving quickly.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building a design system from the ground up.

 - Familiarity with Tokens Studio and design-to-code tooling.

 - Motion and interaction design experience, with a strong sense of when movement improves clarity.

 - Enjoy working beyond product into brand and visual identity.

 - Previous startup experience.

source_jobs.title: UI/UX Designer
source_jobs.description:
WHY WE EXIST

At algo1, we're reimagining one of the world's most overlooked digital experiences: grocery shopping.

Every week, billions of people make hundreds of decisions in stores, often distracted, in a hurry, and with little patience for technology that gets in their way. We believe AI can transform this experience, but only if it's paired with exceptional UX and design.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a UI/UX Designer with exceptional instincts, strong visual craft, and an obsession with interactive design.

This role sits equally across UX thinking and UI execution. One day you might be simplifying a complex decision-making flow; the next you'll be refining typography, spacing and motion until every interaction feels natural.

 - You'll work directly alongside our Head of Design to shape the end-to-end shopper experience, from early concepts through to polished production interfaces.

 - You'll design flows, interactions, prototypes and systems that solve real problems for real shoppers inside real stores.

 - You'll help evolve the design system that underpins the product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design intuitive end-to-end product experiences, from discovery and concept exploration through to polished production UI.

 - Turn complex user problems into simple, elegant interaction flows.

 - Create high-fidelity interfaces with exceptional attention to typography, spacing, hierarchy and visual detail.

 - Build interactive prototypes to communicate ideas, validate assumptions and refine experiences before development.

 - Use motion and micro-interactions intentionally to improve clarity, feedback and delight.

 - Partner closely with engineers to ensure the quality of shipped experiences matches the quality of the design.

 - Contribute to and evolve our design system, including components, tokens, patterns and documentation.

 - Use AI throughout your workflow to explore concepts, generate ideas, prototype faster and improve design quality.

 - Observe real shoppers using the product in stores and translate behavioural insights into better experiences.





WHAT GREAT LOOKS LIKE


ESSENTIAL

 - 3–5 years designing digital products across UX and UI.

 - A portfolio demonstrating thoughtful interaction design and beautifully crafted interfaces.

 - Strong user experience skills with the ability to simplify complex workflows.

 - Excellent visual design fundamentals, including typography, layout, spacing, colour and hierarchy.

 - Experience building and evolving design systems using reusable components, tokens and patterns.

 - Highly proficient in Figma, prototyping and modern product design workflows.

 - Strong understanding of interaction design, usability and accessibility principles.

 - Comfortable using AI as part of your everyday design process.

 - Thrives in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where shipping and learning happen quickly.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience creating or scaling a design system from the ground up.

 - Motion design skills with a strong understanding of meaningful animation and interaction.

 - Familiarity with Tokens Studio, design-to-code workflows and modern frontend collaboration.

 - Experience contributing beyond product into brand systems and visual identity.

 - Previous startup experience.

 - Experience designing AI-powered or consumer-facing products.




OFFICE IS OUR DEFAULT

This is primarily an office-based role for someone who enjoys sketching ideas together, reviewing prototypes side by side, and rapidly iterating with designers and engineers throughout the day.

Our office overlooks what we think is London's best garden square, just a couple of minutes from Tottenham Court Road.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary backed by leading venture capital investors.

 - Meaningful equity in a company building for the long term.

 - 25 days' annual leave plus bank holidays.

 - The opportunity to define the UX of a product millions of people could use every week.

notes: VC-backed stealth-mode company building behavioural AI solutions for retail industry
